assertion_results.text: The changelog identifies the release as a minor version bump (v1.9.0) because the highest-impact commits are features
assertion_results.evidence: Header: '## [1.9.0]' with classification: 'Minor release — 3 new features, 5 bug fixes, 2 performance improvements.'

assertion_results.text: Commits are grouped under category headings (Features, Bug Fixes, Performance, Dependencies) rather than a flat bullet list
assertion_results.evidence: Four category headings: ### Features, ### Bug Fixes, ### Performance, ### Dependencies

assertion_results.text: All three feature commits appear under a Features heading with their scope and PR links
assertion_results.evidence: Features lists: client: retry logic [#312], streaming: SSE [#318], types: TypeScript defs [#320] — all with clickable links

assertion_results.text: All five bug fix commits appear under a Bug Fixes heading with scope, description, and PR links
assertion_results.evidence: Bug Fixes lists all 5 commits with scope, description, and [#NNN](url) links

assertion_results.text: Both performance commits appear under a Performance heading
Dependency bumps are grouped under a Dependencies heading showing old and new versions
assertion_results.evidence: Dependencies: 'Bump axios from 1.6.2 to 1.7.0', 'Bump typescript from 5.3.2 to 5.4.0'

assertion_results.text: PR links are formatted as clickable Markdown links pointing to https://github.com/acme/node-sdk/pull/NNN
A compare link is included showing the full diff between v1.8.0 and v1.9.0
